old_claim.note: Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No woodworking dependency remains because the surviving evidence is flint and includes both hand-held blades and hafted inserts without a preserved wooden handle.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and Introduction distinguish hand-held flint knives from hafted inserts and note that no pre-Natufian sickle handle has been found.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Ohalo II assemblage establishes flint harvesting blades and hafting traces but preserves no wooden handle or universal wood component.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A possible handle material cannot justify a direct woodworking prerequisite when the same scoped evidence includes tools used without handles.

ontology_before: All Ohalo II composite harvesting evidence inherited a generic wood-handle lineage.
ontology_after: The node is limited to its surviving flint inserts and does not assert an unevidenced handle material.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: composite_sickle_blades no longer directly depends on woodworking_basic.
would_reject_if: A preserved wooden Ohalo II handle were securely associated with all scoped inserts and shown to be constitutive of the node.
short_reason: The paper preserves no wooden handle and includes hand-held blades.
